surface.name: Manual Node.js FLUX.1 Schnell run
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Manual Node.js FLUX.1 Schnell run. The Node.js slowstart exposes every local action and ends with two explicit console messages plus output.png. It is chosen as a transparent complete candidate, not as a platform-wide default over scaffold, Python, Colab, fine-tuning, or custom deployment routes. Prior surface before policy commit: Replicate Get started route selector.

alternatives_considered.name: Run a model from Node.js
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: One of three top-level run-model routes; it offers both a one-command scaffold and a manual slowstart but is not labeled the universal default.

alternatives_considered.name: Run a model from Google Colab
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A browser notebook route with no local setup, but the docs home gives it the same Get started level as Node.js and Python.

alternatives_considered.name: Run a model from Python
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A full local Python route, presented alongside Node.js and Colab without a default label.

alternatives_considered.name: Fine-tune an image model
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A different first intent that trains a custom model rather than running a published one.

alternatives_considered.name: Deploy a custom model
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A different first intent with model-building and deployment prerequisites.

entry_point.developer_intent: Run a public AI model on Replicate after selecting the platform.
assumed_prior_state: Discovery is complete and the developer has selected Replicate.
No runtime, language, or notebook route has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Both the exact success message and output.png are present.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: For the selected Manual Node.js FLUX.1 Schnell run, run the Node.js script so the terminal prints Image saved as output.png and output.png exists in the app directory.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Node.js shows Running the model followed by Image saved as output.png and reports the developer is up and running, and output.png exists in the app directory.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Both the exact success message and output.png are present.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The selected Node.js route ends when the script writes output.png and prints the success message. Replicate documents peer Node.js, Python, and Colab routes; the workshop policy commits to the Node.js run and the platform-level route ambiguity is preserved in uncertainties.

prerequisites.requirement: A Replicate account is required to receive the default API token created with the account and to configure billing when the selected model or account requires it.

Billing is conditional for the first request: Replicate allows select models to run free for a limited amount of use, while other usage requires the account's documented prepaid-credit or payment-in-arrears billing model.
A 40-character API token beginning r8_ must be copied from the API tokens page and stored as REPLICATE_API_TOKEN.
Node.js requires Node 16 or greater; local Python requires Python and pip; Colab requires a browser and access to Google Colab instead of local installation.

primary_path.action: Open Replicate Documentation and locate the Get started cards.
details: The page presents Run a model from Node.js, Run a model from Google Colab, Run a model from Python, Fine-tune an image model, and Deploy a custom model.
primary_path.success_signal: All five Get started surfaces are visible.

primary_path.action: Choose the intended first outcome and runtime route before continuing.
details: For running a published model, choose Node.js, Google Colab, or Python.
Fine-tuning and deploying a custom model are different outcomes with larger journeys.
primary_path.success_signal: A human selects the canonical route for the workshop record.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No official default resolves this choice.

primary_path.action: Install or verify Node.js 16 or later.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An older or unavailable Node.js runtime must be upgraded before the quickstart.

primary_path.action: Create a Replicate account or log in.
details: Account creation produces a default API token.
primary_path.success_signal: Account settings and the API tokens page are accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Identity or login recovery must complete before a token can be copied.

primary_path.action: If Replicate prompts for billing for this model or account, complete the assigned billing setup before running the prediction.
details: Some models allow limited free use. Prepaid accounts add a payment method and credit; payment-in-arrears accounts retain a payment method and are charged for usage later.
primary_path.output: Account permitted to run the selected model
primary_path.success_signal: The account can submit black-forest-labs/flux-schnell predictions.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A prepaid account with zero balance cannot start new work. Add credit and confirm the balance updates before retrying.

primary_path.action: Open the API tokens page and copy the default token or create and copy a dedicated token.
details: Replicate tokens are 40 characters and begin with r8_. A dedicated token should be named for its application or environment.
primary_path.success_signal: The full token has been copied and stored securely.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A token that has been exposed must be disabled and replaced before use.

primary_path.action: Export the copied value as REPLICATE_API_TOKEN in the shell.
primary_path.success_signal: REPLICATE_API_TOKEN is set for the Node.js process.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing, malformed, disabled, or wrong-account token causes authentication failure and must be replaced or re-exported.

primary_path.action: Create my-replicate-app and change into it.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Filesystem or permission errors must be corrected.

primary_path.action: Run npm init -y, then npm pkg set type=module.
primary_path.success_signal: package.json exists and declares type module.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: npm or filesystem errors block package initialization.

primary_path.success_signal: replicate is present in package.json and node_modules.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package, network, lockfile, or permission errors must be corrected before code execution.

primary_path.action: Create index.js with the quickstart code that imports Replicate and fs, initializes Replicate, prints Running the model..., calls black-forest-labs/flux-schnell with the astronaut riding a rainbow unicorn prompt, writes the returned image to output.png, and prints Image saved as output.png.
primary_path.success_signal: index.js contains the model call, output stream handling, file write, and both log statements.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Syntax, model identifier, prompt, or file-write errors must be corrected before execution.

primary_path.success_signal: The terminal prints Running the model....
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication, billing, model, network, API, or JavaScript errors stop the run. Correct the reported issue and rerun; failed-run charges depend on the model and deployment type.

primary_path.action: Wait for Replicate to run the model and return its image output.
primary_path.success_signal: The script receives output and completes the file write.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Prediction execution is an external wait. A failed or canceled prediction requires reviewing its error and retrying after the underlying problem is fixed.

primary_path.action: Verify the terminal prints Image saved as output.png and that output.png exists in my-replicate-app.
details: The quickstart says this means the developer is up and running.
primary_path.success_signal: Both the exact success message and output.png are present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the model completed but no file exists, inspect stream handling and filesystem errors, correct index.js, and rerun.

branches.path: Install Node.js 16 or greater; create or log in to Replicate; configure billing only if the selected model or account asks for it; obtain the default or another API token; run npx create-replicate. The package creates a project directory, writes starter code, installs dependencies, and runs it. Verify the resulting working project generates images with FLUX.1 Schnell. Optionally pass --model to choose another model.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a working generated-image Node project through a one-command scaffold, but hides the manual project actions.

branches.path: Install Node.js 16 or greater; complete account and token setup, configuring billing only if prompted for the selected model or account; export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN; create and enter my-replicate-app; run npm init -y and npm pkg set type=module; install replicate; create index.js; initialize Replicate; call black-forest-labs/flux-schnell with the astronaut-on-rainbow-unicorn prompt; write the returned output to output.png; run node index.js; verify Running the model and Image saved as output.png; then stop before Next steps.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Makes every local project action visible and ends at the same generated-image result.

branches.path: Complete account and token setup, configuring billing only if prompted for the selected model or account; install the replicate package with pip; copy the generated token from the API token page and export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN; write Python that imports replicate, runs black-forest-labs/flux-schnell with the iguana-on-the-beach pointillism prompt, saves output[0] to output.png, and prints Image saved as output.png; execute the code and verify the file message. Stop before local-file, URL-input, streaming-output, and other handling examples.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Produces a generated FLUX image through Python rather than a Node project.

branches.path: Open the linked official Replicate Python notebook in Google Colab, complete Replicate account and API-token setup, configure billing only if the selected model or account requires it, run the notebook cells, and verify the selected public model's output. The Replicate documentation page explains Colab and links the notebook but does not enumerate those notebook cells or show a terminal output itself.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids local Python installation but introduces Google Colab access and an external notebook sequence that needs separate capture.

branches.condition: Fine-tune or custom deployment is the intended first outcome.
branches.path: Use the corresponding Get started card and reconstruct its separate training or deployment journey instead of any published-model run route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the developer intent, prerequisites, cost, duration, and terminal result.

branches.condition: An additional API token is desired instead of the account's default token.
branches.path: Open account API tokens, create a token with a descriptive name for the application or environment, copy it, and store it in an environment variable or secrets manager. If exposed, disable it from the same page and replace it.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds token naming and creation but improves separation from other environments.

branches.condition: The selected model is one of Replicate's free-to-run models and the account remains within its initial free allowance.
branches.path: Run the selected model without first adding a payment method or purchasing credit. Continue until Replicate asks the account to set up billing after some free use; features that require billing remain unavailable.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Allows a selected first new-account request to succeed without treating payment or prepaid credit as universal.

branches.condition: The account is assigned prepaid-credit billing or billing is requested after the free allowance.
branches.path: Open Billing settings, add a payment method, select Add credit, choose a purchase amount, and complete the immediate charge; confirm the credit balance updates. Optionally configure auto reload, whose minimum threshold is $5 and minimum reload balance is $15. When the balance reaches zero, Replicate prevents new work and shuts down infrastructure it is running for the account. If a prediction rarely runs beyond the remaining balance, Replicate charges the default payment method at month end for the outstanding amount.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a payment method, an upfront charge, balance checks, optional auto reload, and a hard zero-balance execution gate.

branches.condition: The account is assigned payment-in-arrears billing.
branches.path: Add or retain the configured payment method; use the model during the month; at the beginning of the following month, Replicate charges the previous month's usage. If usage crosses certain limits for the first time, Replicate may charge early and email a receipt, with that amount appearing as a credit on the next invoice.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes the upfront credit purchase but retains usage billing and a possible early fraud-prevention charge.

branches.condition: The prediction fails or is canceled.
branches.path: For an ordinary public or official model, a failed run is not charged; canceling an official model may still be charged, and time-priced models are charged for runtime before cancellation. For private models and deployments, failed and canceled runs are billed for instance active time as normal. A model that calls downstream models can incur downstream-model costs before a root-model failure.
branches.effect_on_first_success: A failed first run can require a retry and may still create cost depending on model ownership, deployment type, downstream calls, and whether cancellation happened after compute started.

friction_gates.description: The official docs require an outcome and Node.js, Colab, or Python route choice without naming a universal default.
The API token and billing flow require a Replicate account; public docs do not enumerate signup screens.

Billing is conditional: selected models have limited free use; prepaid accounts require a payment method and upfront credit; arrears accounts are charged after use. Zero prepaid balance prevents new work and shuts down running infrastructure.
The secret API token must be copied and set in the selected environment; automatically detected exposed tokens are disabled.
Public-model requests can encounter cold boots, shared queues, and scaling limits; setup can take a few seconds while weights are downloaded.

sources.title: Replicate Documentation
evidence_supported: Run, fine-tune, and deploy outcome distinction

sources.title: Run a model from Node.js
evidence_supported: Complete scaffold and manual Node paths

sources.title: Run a model from Google Colab
Billing
evidence_supported: Cold boot, queue, and setup waits

sources.title: Prepaid credit
sections_used: What happens when you run out of credit
evidence_supported: Payment method and Add credit actions

uncertainties.question: Which official Replicate Get started route should define the canonical workshop journey?
uncertainties.impact: The answer changes runtime, installation, project setup, external account dependencies, and the exact output signal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Node.js, Google Colab, and Python are separate top-level run-model routes, while fine-tune and deploy cover other plausible first outcomes; no universal default is named.

uncertainties.question: What are the exact Replicate account signup screens and Google Colab notebook cells?
uncertainties.impact: These can add identity, verification, notebook authorization, secret-entry, execution, and output steps.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official public documentation references account creation and an external notebook but does not enumerate either interactive sequence.

excluded_after_success: Choosing a canonical route without human direction
Node next-step model substitution and web app guides
Python local-file, URL-input, streaming, and file-output examples after the first saved image
Fine-tuning and custom deployment unless selected as the intended first outcome
Production token rotation and auto-reload configuration
